2026-04-17T03:45:12.0340425Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T03:45:12.0340848Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T03:45:12.0341173Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T03:45:12.0341467Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T03:45:12.0341721Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T03:45:12.0341906Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T03:45:12.0342198Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-17T03:45:12.0342342Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T03:45:12.0342487Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-17T03:45:12.0342670Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-17T03:45:12.0343044Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T03:45:12.0343284Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T03:45:12.0343487Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-17T03:47:48.0511992Z 
2026-04-17T03:47:48.0515020Z The issue covers four gaps introduced by PR #58252:
2026-04-17T03:47:49.1714722Z 1. **Chat isolation key** (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key`) — new security feature with zero README coverage
2026-04-17T03:47:49.1716481Z 2. **Logging section** — `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` and HttpClient instrumentation are undocumented
2026-04-17T03:47:49.9405917Z 3. **Stale troubleshooting** — cancel/delete error codes changed (now 404 not 400) but README wasn't updated
2026-04-17T03:47:49.9409405Z 4. **Metadata constraints** — new validation rules (≤16 keys, ≤64-char keys, ≤512-char values) not mentioned

2026-04-17T03:47:53.7272024Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7272181Z   1. Chat isolation key enforcement
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7272413Z   2. HttpClient instrumentation for observability
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7273089Z   3. Structured logging with FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy

2026-04-17T03:47:53.7273678Z   5. Error shape alignment

2026-04-17T03:47:53.7274439Z

2026-04-17T03:47:53.7276217Z ◆ Let me look at the README for the Responses package to see what's currently there and what might be missing, particularly around:
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7276685Z   1. Chat isolation key (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key`)
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7276951Z   2. Observability/HttpClient instrumentation
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7277187Z   3. Error shapes and spec compliance behavior
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7277338Z

2026-04-17T03:47:53.7277696Z 
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7277827Z ✓ view(path: /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-net/azur...)
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7277994Z 
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7278393Z ◆ The README contains a "Troubleshooting" section with some error descriptions. After this PR, there are several things missing from the README:
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7278828Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7279400Z   1. **Chat isolation key (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key`)** — This is a key new security/isolation feature that's completely absent from the README. There's no documentation about:
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7279982Z      - How to use `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7280236Z      - What happens when keys mismatch
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7280494Z      - How `ResponseContext.Isolation` exposes this key
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7280719Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7280999Z   2. **Observability/Logging** — The logging section is minimal. After this PR:
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7281454Z      - `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` was added (logs outbound Foundry API calls with correlation headers)
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7281866Z      - HttpClient instrumentation was added for OTLP tracing
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7282324Z      - The README only mentions the activity source but doesn't cover the new Foundry storage logging or HttpClient instrumentation
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7282727Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7283474Z   3. **Troubleshooting — error shapes** — The error descriptions for cancellation are outdated, particularly around what happens when attempting to cancel a response that's already in a terminal state.
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7284053Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7284468Z   4. **Metadata constraints** — No documentation on the 16-pair limit, 64-character key limit, or 512-character value limit.
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7284847Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7285147Z   5. **Response ID validation** — Missing details on how malformed response IDs are handled.
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7285441Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7286173Z   The biggest gaps are the undocumented chat isolation key feature, the incomplete observability documentation, and the stale troubleshooting section. I should prioritize filing an issue for the chat isolation key and observability gaps since those have the most impact on users.
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7286902Z 
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7287127Z ✓ view(path: /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-net/azur..., view_range: [188, 220])
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7287357Z 
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7287528Z ✓ glob(pattern: sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Res...)
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7287713Z 
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7288178Z ◆ Now I have a clear picture of the documentation gaps. Let me file a GitHub issue describing the gaps. The main gaps are:
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7288557Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7289227Z   1. **Chat isolation key** — New security feature `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` is completely undocumented in the README. There's no section explaining how to use it, what it does, or what errors to expect on mismatch.
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7289968Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7290305Z   2. **Observability/Logging section** — Only mentions OpenTelemetry activity source. Missing:
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7290930Z      - `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` (new in this PR) — logs outbound Foundry storage calls with correlation headers
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7291451Z      - HttpClient instrumentation for outbound trace correlation (new in this PR)
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7291768Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7292743Z   3. **Troubleshooting — stale error descriptions** — The cancel troubleshooting text says "400 Bad Request (cancel): ...already reached a terminal state" but the spec-compliant behavior now uses `invalid_request_error` type with specific messages. Also cancel-after-delete now returns 404.
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7293718Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7294215Z   4. **Metadata constraints** — `metadata` maps now validated (≤16 keys, key ≤64 chars, value ≤512 chars); not mentioned in README.
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7294986Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7295612Z   5. **Response ID format requirements** — The README doesn't mention that response IDs (path param) have specific format constraints.
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7296215Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7297217Z   The chat isolation key is the biggest gap here since it's a new security feature for multi-tenant deployments that developers absolutely need to know about. I should file an issue to track these documentation updates.
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7298100Z 
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7298538Z ✓ safeoutputs-create_issue(title: [AgentServer] Docs: Document chat isolat..., body: ## Documentation Gap
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7299092Z 
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7299253Z **Package:** `Azur..., temporary_id: aw_agsvdoc1)
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7299486Z 
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7299659Z ✓ safeoutputs-dispatch_triage
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7299877Z

2026-04-17T03:47:53.7301309Z   
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7301614Z   The issue covers four gaps introduced by PR #58252:
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7302385Z   1. **Chat isolation key** (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key`) — new security feature with zero README coverage
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7303231Z   2. **Logging section** — `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` and HttpClient instrumentation are undocumented
2026-04-17T03:47:53.7303869Z   3. **Stale troubleshooting** — cancel/delete error codes changed (now 404 not 400) but README wasn't updated

2026-04-17T03:47:53.8985219Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T03:46:19.962386197Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"[{\"sha\":\"[REDACTED]\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]\",\"commit\":{\"message\":\"[AgentServer] Spec compliance, chat isolation, and observability (#58252)\\n\\n* Eager eviction of completed ResponseExecution from tracker\\n\\nReplace MarkCompleted (which kept completed executions in memory forever)\\nwith TryEvict (which removes from the dictionary immediately after\\nfinalization). This fixes a memory leak where completed executions\\naccumulated indefinitely in the ResponseExecutionTracker.\\n\\nAfter eviction, all API calls (GET, DELETE, Cancel, SSE replay) for\\ncompleted responses fall through to the storage provider, which is the\\ncorrect source of truth for persisted data.\\n\\nKey changes:\\n- ResponseExecutionTracker: replace MarkCompleted with TryEvict\\n- ResponseExecution: remove unused CompletedAt property\\n- ResponseOrchestrator: simplified guards (all tracked are in-flight)\\n- ResponseEndpointHandler: added provider-path SSE replay guard\\n- SseReplayResult: catch BadRequestException from stream provider\\n- InMemoryResponsesProvider: throw BadRequestException (not IOE) for\\n  missing event streams — gives honest error for both 'never had stream'\\n  and 'stream TTL expired' cases\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Align error shapes with container spec (TDD)\\n\\n- Default code to 'invalid_request_error' in InvalidRequest() and NotFound()\\n- Cancel incomplete: 'Cannot cancel a response in terminal state.'\\n- SSE replay: split into distinct messages for background/stream with param\\n- Delete in-flight: 'Cannot delete an in-flight response.'\\n- Tests updated first (TDD red/green) across 8 test files\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Spec compliance: post-delete 404, C6/usage/cancel-after-delete tests\\n\\nAlign with responses-api-behaviour-contract.md Post-Deletion Behaviour:\\n- GET/input_items/cancel after DELETE now return 404 (was 400)\\n- InMemoryResponsesProvider tombstone checks throw ResourceNotFoundException\\n\\nNew protocol tests closing audit gaps:\\n- C6 (store=false + stream=true + no-bg): SSE stream completes, GET 404\\n- Cancel after DELETE: returns 404 per spec\\n- Token usage B33: handler-provided ResponseUsage appears in JSON\\n  response body, SSE response.completed event, and GET on background\\n  responses; absent usage serializes as null\\n\\nAll 1872 tests pass.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Integrate ID + metadata validation into validator pipeline (B40, B29)\\n\\n- Fix spec typo: 'Malformed identitifer.' → 'Malformed identifier.'\\n- Move previous_response_id format validation into\\n  CreateResponsePayloadValidatorCustom (B29 pipeline, code: invalid_value,\\n  param: $.previous_response_id)\\n- Move metadata constraints (16 pairs, 64-char keys, 512-char values) into\\n  MetadataValidatorCustom (B29 pipeline)\\n- Remove duplicate handler-level ValidateMetadata() and previous_response_id\\n  checks from ResponseEndpointHandler\\n- Path-parameter B40 validation stays in handler (code: invalid_parameters,\\n  param: responseId{\\u003cvalue\\u003e})\\n- Update all tests using fake 'resp_*' or short 'caresp_*' IDs to use\\n  IdGenerator.NewResponseId() to avoid B40 rejections\\n- Add 3 new test files: MalformedIdProtocolTests (7 tests),\\n  MetadataConstraintProtocolTests (8 tests),\\n  CreationIdValidationProtocolTests (3 tests)\\n\\nAll 1890 tests pass.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add chat isolation key enforcement across all endpoints\\n\\nWhen a response is created with a chat isolation key (x-agent-chat-isolation-key),\\nall subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls must provide the same key.\\nMismatch or missing key returns standard 404 (indistinguishable from not-found)\\nto prevent cross-chat information leakage. No enforcement when created without a key.\\n\\nEnforcement points:\\n- In-flight (tracker): orchestrator GetAsync/CancelAsync, handler SSE GET/DELETE\\n- Persisted (provider): InMemoryResponsesProvider Get/Delete/InputItems\\n\\nImplementation:\\n- ResponseExecution.ChatIsolationKey: stores creation-time key for in-flight checks\\n- EnforceInFlightChatIsolation(): shared helper in handler and orchestrator\\n- InMemoryResponsesProvider._chatIsolationKeys: ConcurrentDictionary for persisted checks\\n\\n17 new E2E protocol tests covering all endpoints (match/mismatch/missing),\\nin-flight background responses, and error body verification.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add HttpClient instrumentation for outbound trace correlation\\n\\nRegister AddHttpClientInstrumentation() for both tracing and metrics in\\nthe OTLP-only path (non-Azure Monitor). This exports outbound HTTP client\\nspans so distributed traces show the full call chain through the Foundry\\nstorage layer. UseAzureMonitor already registers both instrumentations,\\nso the existing guard prevents duplicate spans.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Update changelogs for Responses beta.2 and Core beta.22\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add structured logging to FoundryStorageProvider\\n\\nUse LoggerMessage source-generated methods to log every outbound Foundry\\nstorage API call with operation name, response ID, HTTP status code, and\\nduration. Debug level for success, Warning for HTTP errors, Error for\\nexceptions. Never logs credentials or PII.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Replace inline logging with FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy\\n\\nMove Foundry storage request/response logging from FoundryStorageProvider\\ninto a dedicated HttpPipeline per-retry policy. This captures correlation\\nheaders on both sides:\\n- Request: x-ms-client-request-id (stamped by Azure.Core)\\n- Response: x-ms-request-id (returned by Foundry service)\\n\\nEach retry attempt is logged independently with method, URI, status code,\\nand duration. FoundryStorageProvider reverts to its clean form with no\\nlogging dependency.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Log x-request-id and apim-request-id correlation headers\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Raise storage success log level to Information\\n\\nCo-authored-by: ResponseExecution.EnforceChatIsolation()\\n  instance method, removing duplication from handler and orchestrator\\n- Add null guard for execution.Response in GET path (pre-response.created race)\\n-
